top10.name: SAT-CHI-0045

Its primary mission is to deliver precise positioning and navigation signals, serving military and defence agencies. The satellite occupies a geostationary orbit (GEO) at ~35,786 km, maintaining a fixed position over Earth on a Sun-Synchronous trajectory, with an orbital inclination of 10.1° and a period of 1438.4 minutes. Its orbit is nearly perfectly circular, ensuring consistent coverage. It forms a critical node in the global navigation satellite system (GNSS), enabling centimetre-level positioning accuracy for aviation, maritime, and terrestrial users.
